methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $141M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
MacroFab is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $141M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine companies in the robotics and manufacturing sector at the same or adjacent stages, comprising eight private firms and one public comparable (277810.KQ). Key peers include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy, applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $240M. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company comp set produces $1.6B, with an observed range from $300M to $10.0B. The peer median reflects substantial variation driven by differences in capital efficiency, market positioning, and stage maturity within the robotics sector.

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $204M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $920M. The 9x spread between low and high estimates reflects genuine uncertainty around MacroFab's relative position within a highly dispersed peer set. Key variables affecting placement within this range include revenue scale, gross margins, capital efficiency relative to peers, and the strength of customer concentration and pipeline visibility.
